info.description: Personal health data API. Aggregates Oura, Strava, Hevy, InBody, Apple Health, and meal logs. Every endpoint is also exposed as an LLM tool — see /openapi.json, /tools/openai.json, /tools/anthropic.json.
info.title: Health Connect API

post.description: Runs a Claude analysis over the daily summary and returns 3–4 specific bullet-point insights. Defaults to yesterday if `date` is not provided. Use this when the user wants commentary or interpretation rather than raw data.
post.summary: Generate AI health insights for a day

parameters.description: Analysis request — date is optional and defaults to yesterday
parameters.name: body

post.description: Accepts a HealthKit export payload (typically from a Shortcuts/Auto Export script) and persists weight + vitals records. Use for backfilling or scheduled pushes from a phone.

get.description: Returns vital sign readings (heart rate, resting HR, blood pressure, etc.) for a metric across an optional date range.
Returns weight readings (kg) and optional body composition fields over an optional date range. Sourced from Apple Health, manual entries, or InBody pushes.

post.description: Records a manual or device-sourced weight reading. Use this when the user wants to log their weight or body composition.

get.description: Returns Hevy gym workouts (each with its sets) over an optional date range. Use this for strength training questions: volume, sets, reps, exercises lifted.
Returns a single gym workout with its full set list.
get.summary: Get one Hevy workout by id

get.description: Returns InBody scans (weight, body fat %, muscle mass, etc.) over an optional date range. Use for body composition trends.

parameters.description: Maximum number of scans to return (newest first)
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Returns the single most recent InBody body composition scan, or 404 if none exist.
get.summary: Get the most recent InBody scan

get.description: Returns logged meals filtered by an exact date or a date range. Use for nutrition questions: calories, macros, what was eaten on a date.

parameters.description: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD), inclusive — ignored if date is set
parameters.name: from

parameters.description: End date (YYYY-MM-DD), inclusive — ignored if date is set
parameters.name: to

post.description: Creates a meal log entry. Use this when the user wants to record what they ate.

delete.description: Removes a meal entry by id.

get.description: Returns daily Oura scores (sleep, readiness, activity, HRV) over a date range. Use this for trend questions or when the user asks for readiness/activity/sleep scores.
Returns the daily Oura score (sleep, readiness, activity) for one calendar day.
get.summary: Get Oura scores for a specific day

get.description: Returns sleep sessions from the Oura Ring within an optional date range and type filter. Use this when the user asks how they slept, sleep duration, sleep stages, or HRV during sleep.

parameters.description: Sleep type filter: long, short, or nap
parameters.name: type

get.description: Returns sleep sessions recorded for a single calendar day. Use this when the user asks about a specific date's sleep.
get.summary: Get Oura sleep for a specific day

get.description: Returns Strava activities (runs, rides, swims) within an optional date range and activity type filter. Use this for questions about workouts, distance, pace, or cardio output.

parameters.description: Activity type filter (e.g. Run, Ride, Swim, Walk)
parameters.name: type

get.description: Returns full detail for one Strava activity including pace, heart rate, and elevation if available.
get.summary: Get a single Strava activity by id

get.description: Aggregates Oura, Strava, Hevy, InBody, Apple Health, and meals into a single daily summary. Use this as the default starting point when the user asks "how was my day" or "summarize my health on date X".

parameters.description: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD), inclusive — ignored if date is set
parameters.name: from

parameters.description: End date (YYYY-MM-DD), inclusive — ignored if date is set
parameters.name: to

get.description: Returns a rolling-window report ending at `date` covering the past `lookback` days (default 30). Use for "give me a report" requests.

parameters.description: End date of the report (YYYY-MM-DD); defaults to today
parameters.name: date

parameters.description: Number of days to look back from date (default 30)
parameters.name: lookback

get.description: Returns weekly aggregates (averages and totals across sources). Use this when the user asks for week-over-week trends.
Returns last successful sync timestamp, last status, and total records added per source.

post.description: Forces a sync for the named source. Pass "all" to sync every registered source. Use when the user wants their data refreshed before analysis.

parameters.description: Source name: oura, strava, hevy, inbody, or all
parameters.name: source

BearerAuth.description: Bearer token. Set the same value in HEALTH_CONNECT_API_TOKEN on the server.
BearerAuth.name: Authorization
